title: Active Inference Agent Simulations: Adaptive Advantages of Somatic Markers
description: Computational agent simulations test whether somatic marker integration M̂(c,a) = γ_V·V(c,a) + γ_A·A(c,a) confers measurable adaptive advantages over APGI agents lacking somatic marker function. Agents are evaluated on embodied decision tasks under volatility conditions where rapid threshold adaptation is required. Four sub-predictions are tested: that somatic-marker APGI agents outperform lesion agents on reward accumulation under volatility (P3a), that post-ignition action selection diversifies relative to pre-ignition baseline (P3b), that vmPFC proxy activation (M̂ retrieval signal) temporally precedes threshold crossing (P3c), and that removing the β parameter via lesion simulation produces specific performance deficits not observed in other parameter lesions (P3d). SUBMISSION-BLOCKING: APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py is required for full validation of M̂ identifiability and is not yet built.

sub_predictions.claim: APGI agent with somatic markers outperforms lesion agent on embodied tasks
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Somatic-marker agent achieves higher cumulative reward under volatility conditions (reward ratio > 1.15, permutation p < 0.05)
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No statistically significant performance advantage across all volatility conditions

sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Post-ignition action selection entropy increases relative to pre-ignition baseline (paired Wilcoxon, p < 0.05)
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Ignition events uncorrelated with subsequent action diversity or adaptation speed

sub_predictions.claim: Somatic marker retrieval M̂ temporally precedes threshold crossing
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Cross-correlation peak between M̂ activation and θₜ crossing is negative-lag (M̂ leads by ≥ 1 trial in ≥ 75% of ignition events)
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: M̂ activation simultaneous with or lagging threshold crossing

sub_predictions.claim: Removing β specifically degrades performance; other parameter lesions do not produce equivalent deficit
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: β-lesion agent shows significantly lower reward than Πⁱ-lesion and α-lesion agents under matched volatility; effect specific to somatic modulation pathway
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: β lesion produces equivalent or lesser deficit compared to other single-parameter lesions

validation_notes: Computationally specified. APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py required for full validation of M̂(c,a) parameter identifiability. This script is not yet built. Submission-blocking for Paper 1.

inclusion_criteria: Synthetic agent: computational simulation only — no human participants
Agent initialised with APGI parameters within physiologically plausible bounds
Environment: stochastic multi-armed bandit with volatility schedule (σ_env ∈ {0.1, 0.3, 0.6})
exclusion_criteria: Simulation runs with numerical instability (S_t > 1e6 or NaN values) excluded
Runs where convergence not achieved within 500 trials excluded from analysis

apparatus.agent_framework: Custom APGI active inference agent (src/apgi/core.py + somatic marker extension)
apparatus.environment: Stochastic multi-armed bandit (3 arms) with hidden state volatility; reversal probability ∈ {0.02, 0.05, 0.10} per trial
comparison_conditions: Full APGI agent (with somatic marker M̂, β ∈ [0.3, 0.8])
Πⁱ-lesion agent (Πⁱ_eff = constant, no metabolic gating)
apparatus.random_seeds: 1000 independent seeds per condition for bootstrapped confidence intervals

target.type: Computational trial: agent selects action from {1, 2, 3}, receives stochastic reward signal

trial_structure.response: Agent selects action via softmax policy over precision-weighted prediction errors

blocks.volatility_schedule: Block-wise: σ_env = [0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 0.3, 0.1]; reversal schedule pseudorandom within block

somatic_marker_model.V_ca: Anticipated value of action a in context c (learned via temporal difference)
somatic_marker_model.A_ca: Anticipated arousal signal associated with action a in context c (proxy for SCR/heart rate)
somatic_marker_model.identifiability_requirement: Block-diagonal Fisher Information Matrix; M̂ must be orthogonal to raw Πⁱ in fitting procedure — requires APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py

dependent_variables: Cumulative reward per agent type over 500 trials (primary outcome)
Action selection entropy post-ignition vs. pre-ignition (bits)
Cross-correlation lag: M̂ activation vs. θₜ crossing
β-lesion vs. other-lesion reward difference (permutation test)
Ignition rate per agent type per volatility condition
Threshold adaptation speed (trials to re-optimise after reversal) per agent type
primary_hypothesis: Full APGI agents with somatic marker function achieve higher cumulative reward than β-lesion agents under volatile environments, and the performance advantage is specific to the β pathway rather than general precision reduction.
primary_analysis: Non-parametric permutation test (10,000 permutations) for reward advantage: full APGI vs. β-lesion, collapsed across volatility conditions. Secondary: Wilcoxon signed-rank for post- vs. pre-ignition entropy. Cross-correlation analysis (lag −5 to +5 trials) for M̂ vs. Bₜ temporal ordering.

power_analysis.based_on: Damasio somatic marker advantage estimates; active inference agent benchmarks from Friston et al. (2017)

submission_blocking_issues: APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py: Script required to confirm M̂(c,a) parameter identifiability and orthogonality of β and Πⁱ estimates is NOT YET BUILT. This is a submission-blocking gap. Parameter recovery criterion (Pearson r > 0.75 for β and Πⁱ separately) must be demonstrated before Paper 1 submission.
caveats: Somatic marker model M̂(c,a) = γ_V·V(c,a) + γ_A·A(c,a) is one formalisation among several; Damasio's original account does not specify γ_V and γ_A uniquely. The values used here are motivated by interoceptive salience literature but require empirical calibration.
Agent performance advantages depend on volatility magnitude; effect may not replicate in stationary environments.
